text: This study makes a contribution to the known research on the current situation of inclusive education in Ecuador. It reveals the existing structural gap between the political law that establishes the provision of specific physical resources and materials for the educational development of students with disabilities and their effective implementation in educational institutions. This leads to a continuous inequality in relation to the academic and development achievements of these students, who are denied access to educational spaces that are appropriate for their needs. According to the results of the research carried out based on the PRISMA-ScR method and according to a comparative analysis carried out on 16 scientific papers and 11 official documents from countries with renowned inclusive education systems such as Finland, Japan, Canada and the United States, Ecuador is in a rut of institutional lethargy that is difficult to overcome due to the lack of material resources that hinder the progressive application of political law. Therefore, this study highlights that, in addition to the political law regulating the physical accessibility of the schools and the required materials for students with disabilities, the effective implementation of the inclusion of these students implies a complex set of measures, including the standardization of the physical accessibility of the schools, a higher investment in technological devices and a better training of teachers. This paper contrasts local educational practices with the mentioned international standards, identifying the existing possibilities for change in relation to the management of the resources and the school culture, in the Ecuadorian educational system.
